title: [BUG] `Invalid Date` object returned for formula result formatted as date
author: tjallingt
body:
## 🐛 Bug Report

<!-- A clear and concise description of what the bug is. -->

Lib version: 4.4.0

## Steps To Reproduce

Add a formula which results in a date to an excel sheet, then read the resulting value using exceljs

```javascript
const wb = new ExcelJS.Workbook();
const ws = wb.addWorksheet('sheet1');

expect(ws.getCell('A1').value).to.equal(new Date(1757411854508));
```

## The expected behaviour:

The `.result`  value of the formula should be a valid date.
Instead an `Invalid Date` object is returned.

## Possible solution (optional, but very helpful):

The source is these lines here:

https://github.com/exceljs/exceljs/blob/5bed18b45e824f409b08456b59b87430ded023ab/lib/xlsx/xform/sheet/cell-xform.js#L457-L459

`model.result` is a string, for example `"27/08/2025 19:33:34"`, while  `numFmt === "mm-dd-yy"`.
This causes exceljs to try to convert this string into a date which results in it calling `new Date(NaN)`, producing the `Invalid Date` object.

A solution could be to check whether the value is a string and then attempt to turn the string into a date. Alternatively the value could be passed along as a string.

comments.author: jameslol417
comments.body: @tjallingt this issue seems to have been fixed with https://github.com/protobi/exceljs/issues/19 at the @protobi/exceljs fork which applied https://github.com/exceljs/exceljs/pull/2956
